executiveSummary: The CodeWorld Observatory is operating at Phase 19 (Research Prioritization Engine). Current structural posture is pressured. 9 simulation sessions recorded alongside 3 benchmark evaluations. 1 strategy class currently rate as structurally favorable out of 5. Calibration trend is stable. 2 of 6 experiment(s) now expose persisted replay lineage above baseline-only, and 2 have artifact-complete replay-package lineage. Current evidence-weighted leader is Phase 15 lineage smoke at strong comparative weight, with 0 high-confidence comparison(s). Primary advisory priority: Phase 15 progression smoke has replay maturity that outpaces its empirical depth.

replayStatusSummary.reproducibilityCaveat: Full replay-package lineage is present for at least one experiment, but that still means artifact-complete lineage rather than exact historical reconstruction.

summaryCards.label: Composite Posture
summaryCards.detail: Composite posture is pressured: complexity is extreme; dependency pressure is systemic; evidence is thin.

topPriorities.summary: Phase 15 progression smoke has replay maturity that outpaces its empirical depth.
topPriorities.rationale: Phase 15 progression smoke has full replay semantics and full-replay-package lineage, but its empirical depth remains narrow with single-run benchmark coverage. Replay maturity should not be mistaken for broad empirical support.
topPriorities.recommendedFocus: Add repeated benchmark or execution-backed evidence before treating replay maturity as broadly supported.
topPriorities.caveat: Research prioritization ranks structural evidence, replay, calibration, and comparison gaps only. It is advisory, not causal proof, certainty, or a hidden write-side decision system.

sections.title: System State Overview
sections.content: Structural complexity is extreme with 238 files across 11 module boundaries. Dependency pressure is systemic. Validation burden is heavy. Governance friction is smooth.

sections.title: Experiment Coverage
sections.content: 9 simulation sessions and 3 benchmark runs are on record. 6 experiments now expose experiment-centered lineage. Evidence coverage currently stands at 0 strong, 3 adequate, and 0 insufficient benchmark assessments.

sections.title: Replay and Lineage Status
sections.content: 4 experiment(s) remain baseline-only, 0 have stored-evidence-chain replay, and 2 have full-replay-package lineage. Strongest current experiment: Phase 15 progression smoke. Current strongest evidence class is full-replay-package. Full replay-package lineage is present for at least one experiment, but that still means artifact-complete lineage rather than exact historical reconstruction.

prioritization.recommendedFocus: Add repeated benchmark or execution-backed evidence before treating replay maturity as broadly supported.
prioritization.caveat: Research prioritization ranks structural evidence, replay, calibration, and comparison gaps only. It is advisory, not causal proof, certainty, or a hidden write-side decision system.

sections.title: Comparative Experiment Evaluation
sections.content: Phase 15 lineage smoke currently leads the experiment set at strong comparative weight with medium confidence. Phase 15 lineage smoke and Phase 15 progression smoke remain roughly comparable on persisted evidence. Differences are not large enough to justify a stronger ranking. Latest persisted evaluation snapshot: 3/17/2026, 10:36:20 AM. Comparative leader remained Phase 15 lineage smoke. High-confidence comparison count did not change. 0 experiment(s) changed persisted comparative posture. Comparative weights rank persisted evidence completeness and empirical depth only. They do not imply causal proof, exact historical replay, or stronger certainty than the attached artifacts justify.

sections.title: Strategy Landscape
sections.content: 1 strategy class are currently favorable: minimal-touch. 3 strategy classes remain viable with review: route-first, ui-first, and structural-refactor. 1 strategy class are structurally misaligned: service-first.

sections.title: Transition Pattern Memory
sections.content: 2 recurring transition patterns identified across 9 sessions. Dominant patterns include Under moderate complexity, contained pressure, and smooth friction, strategy 'mixed' tends to dominate with 'structurally-favorable' outcomes. (9 occurrences, high confidence); Governance blocker 'Requires explicit manual review. Risk unacceptably high.' recurs across 9 session branch results, indicating a systemic constraint. (9 occurrences, high confidence).

sections.title: Calibration State
sections.content: Prediction-reality calibration covers 3 evaluations. Alignment score is 100.0%, and the trend is stable. Calibration exhibits stable structural alignment across recorded sessions.

findings.title: Pressured Composite Posture
findings.summary: Structural posture remains pressured. Complexity is extreme; dependency pressure is systemic; evidence is thin.

findings.title: 1 Strategy Class Misaligned
findings.summary: The following strategies are structurally misaligned with current repo conditions: service-first. Service-first restructuring is most effective when dependency pressure is contained and module boundaries are clear. Current structural conditions introduce friction that may reduce effectiveness.

constraints.constraint: All predictions are deterministic structural calculations, not learned probabilistic models.
constraints.implication: Prediction quality is bounded by the fidelity of static analysis proxies. No neural world model is active.

constraints.constraint: Calibration loop requires executed interventions with recorded outcomes to produce trend data.
constraints.implication: Systems with no executed interventions cannot derive calibration scores or movement trends.

constraints.constraint: Dependency analysis is limited to static import/require resolution.
constraints.implication: Cross-module runtime event bubbling, dynamic imports, and reflection-based coupling are not captured.

constraints.constraint: Strategy compatibility is computed from threshold-gated structural descriptors, not empirical outcome data.
constraints.implication: Compatibility assessments may diverge from actual intervention success rates as code complexity grows.

constraints.constraint: Full replayability means artifact-complete replay-package lineage, not exact historical branch reconstruction.
constraints.implication: Consumer surfaces must preserve the distinction between artifact completeness and historical certainty.

prioritization.rationale: Phase 15 progression smoke has full replay semantics and full-replay-package lineage, but its empirical depth remains narrow with single-run benchmark coverage. Replay maturity should not be mistaken for broad empirical support.
prioritization.recommendedFocus: Add repeated benchmark or execution-backed evidence before treating replay maturity as broadly supported.
prioritization.caveat: Research prioritization ranks structural evidence, replay, calibration, and comparison gaps only. It is advisory, not causal proof, certainty, or a hidden write-side decision system.

constraints.constraint: Evidence-weighted comparison ranks experiments by persisted evidence classes and empirical depth rather than by causal proof or scientific certainty.
constraints.implication: Comparative leaders can guide prioritization, but low-confidence or roughly comparable pairings should not be overstated as decisive wins.

recommendedNextSteps.action: Prioritize structural complexity with a structural-refactor intervention.
recommendedNextSteps.rationale: 238 files across 11 module boundaries with 1.6 avg edges/file. 21 files at >3 directory depth. Recommended intervention class: structural-refactor.

recommendedNextSteps.action: Attach simulation and benchmark artifacts to baseline-only experiments before interpreting them as replay-ready.
recommendedNextSteps.rationale: Experiment registration alone is not enough for stored-evidence-chain replay. Baseline-only experiments still need persisted simulation and benchmark evidence.

recommendedNextSteps.action: Repeat benchmark and execution capture on the leading experiments before treating current rankings as decisive.
recommendedNextSteps.rationale: Evidence-weighted comparison exists, but the comparative confidence remains too low for stronger interpretation. Additional repeated benchmarks or execution-backed outcomes will raise empirical depth without changing doctrine.

recommendedNextSteps.action: Export the current briefing as a persisted artifact for longitudinal comparison.
recommendedNextSteps.rationale: Persisted briefings enable cross-session posture drift detection and historical audit.

recommendedNextSteps.action: Extend observation encoder to capture cross-module event bus routing and dynamic imports.
recommendedNextSteps.rationale: Current static analysis misses runtime coupling, limiting dependency pressure accuracy.

knownLimitations: This briefing synthesizes from deterministic structural signals. No learned connectionist model is active.
Strategy trends are derived from branch-ID heuristics and first-class metadata - not from validated causal inference.
Evidence sufficiency labels reflect benchmark heuristic coverage, not independent verification.
Evidence-weighted comparisons rank persisted evidence completeness and empirical depth, not causal proof or statistically mature causal effects.
Cross-module runtime event bubbling cannot be captured by static AST proxy layers.
Calibration alignment scores represent structural proximity, not prediction accuracy in the statistical sense.
Full replay-package lineage does not guarantee branch-perfect or historically exact reproduction beyond what captured artifacts justify.
